title: Test suite depends on live repository history and will fail based on checkout state or time
reasoning: npm run test always runs the git tests, and two tests call real git commands against the current checkout. One requires a .git directory, and the other assumes there is a commit with non-zero changes in the last 30 days. That makes the package test command non-hermetic and can break in package extraction, shallow/minimal CI checkouts, or after a quiet month even when the code is correct.
recommendation: Keep parser behavior tested with fixed git-log fixtures, and either mock the git command boundary or move live-repo integration checks behind an explicit opt-in environment flag.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: These lines are themselves the fragile tests; the rest of the suite uses deterministic fixtures and does not verify that npm run test remains independent of local git state.
suggestedRegressionTest: Run the git data tests with the git command mocked to throw or return a fixed fixture, and assert the tested behavior does not require live repository history unless an integration-test flag is set.
minimumFixScope: tests/lib/data/git.test.ts, with a small injectable seam or mock around the git command boundary if needed.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
